Viral Fever.explanation: Paracetamol effectively reduces fever and relieves body pain in viral infections. Stay hydrated and rest well.
lifestyle: Drink at least 8–10 glasses of water or ORS per day
Take complete bed rest for 2–3 days
Eat light, easily digestible food (dal, khichdi, soups)
Monitor temperature every 4 hours
Avoid heavy exercise until fully recovered
Do not self-medicate with antibiotics (viral, not bacterial)
Viral Fever.when_to_seek_care: If fever exceeds 103°F, lasts more than 5 days, or you develop difficulty breathing — seek medical attention immediately.

Bacterial Fever.dosage: Amoxicillin-Clavulanate 625mg twice daily | Paracetamol 500mg every 6 hours
Bacterial Fever.explanation: Bacterial fever requires targeted antibiotic therapy. Amoxicillin-Clavulanate covers a broad spectrum of gram-positive and gram-negative organisms. Always complete the full course.
lifestyle: Complete the full antibiotic course even if fever subsides in 2–3 days
Drink 8–10 glasses of water daily to prevent dehydration
Eat high-protein food to support immune recovery
Monitor temperature every 4 hours — if above 104°F go to hospital
Rest completely and avoid physical exertion
Avoid self-medicating with additional antibiotics
Bacterial Fever.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency if fever exceeds 104°F, you develop confusion, rapid breathing, or cold/clammy skin — these indicate sepsis.

Normal Fever.explanation: A mild fever is usually the body's immune response. Paracetamol helps control temperature and discomfort.
lifestyle: Drink plenty of fluids — water, coconut water, ORS
Rest adequately and avoid strenuous activities
Eat light nutritious food
Monitor temperature regularly
Wear light clothing and keep room well-ventilated
Avoid cold baths — use lukewarm sponging if needed
Normal Fever.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if fever > 103°F, lasts more than 3 days, or accompanied by rash, stiff neck, or breathing trouble.

Common Cold.dosage: Paracetamol every 6 hours | Cetirizine once daily at bedtime
Common Cold.explanation: Paracetamol manages fever and body ache. Cetirizine (antihistamine) relieves runny nose and sneezing. Steam inhalation helps decongest airways.
lifestyle: Steam inhalation twice daily (add eucalyptus oil if available)
Drink warm fluids — herbal tea, warm water with honey and ginger
Rest well — 8+ hours of sleep
Avoid cold beverages and exposure to cold air
Gargle with warm saline water for throat comfort
Wash hands frequently to prevent spreading
Common Cold.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if symptoms worsen after 7 days, high fever develops, or you experience sinus pain or earache.

Influenza.dosage: Oseltamivir 75mg twice daily for 5 days | Paracetamol 500mg every 6 hours
Influenza.explanation: Oseltamivir is an antiviral that reduces flu duration and severity when started within 48 hours of symptom onset. Paracetamol manages fever and body ache. NSAIDs may be used for pain relief.
lifestyle: Isolate yourself to prevent spreading flu to others
Drink warm fluids generously — soups, herbal teas, ORS
Complete bed rest for at least 3–5 days
Monitor temperature every 6 hours
Use mask if going out — flu spreads via droplets
Annual flu vaccination recommended for prevention
Influenza.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency if breathing difficulty develops, chest pain, confusion, or you cannot keep fluids down — especially in elderly, young children, or immunocompromised.

Bronchitis.dosage: Amoxicillin 500mg 3x daily (if bacterial) | Guaifenesin 400mg every 4 hours | Salbutamol 2 puffs PRN
Bronchitis.explanation: Bronchitis involves inflammation of the bronchial tubes. Guaifenesin helps loosen mucus. Antibiotics are used only if sputum is purulent (green/yellow). Salbutamol relieves wheeze.
lifestyle: Steam inhalation with menthol/eucalyptus 3 times daily
Drink 2–3 litres of warm fluids daily to thin mucus
Avoid smoking and passive smoke exposure completely
Elevate head while sleeping to ease breathing
Use humidifier in room if available
Avoid exposure to dust, pollution, cold air — wear a mask
Bronchitis.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if cough lasts more than 3 weeks, sputum is blood-tinged, breathing worsens, or you develop high fever > 103°F.

Dengue Fever.dosage: 500mg every 6 hours — NEVER use Ibuprofen or Aspirin
Dengue Fever.explanation: Dengue is a viral hemorrhagic fever spread by Aedes mosquitoes. Paracetamol controls fever. NSAIDs like Ibuprofen increase bleeding risk and are strictly contraindicated. Platelet monitoring is essential.
lifestyle: Drink oral rehydration solution (ORS) every 2–3 hours
Complete bed rest — no physical exertion
Eat soft foods — soups, khichdi, fruit juice
Monitor platelet count daily (hospital/clinic)
Sleep under mosquito nets and use repellent
Seek immediate care if gums bleed or rash appears
Papaya leaf juice may help support platelet recovery
Dengue Fever.when_to_seek_care: EMERGENCY: Go to hospital immediately if you have bleeding gums, petechiae (red spots on skin), severe abdominal pain, vomiting blood, or platelet count < 50,000.

Typhoid Fever.dosage: Ciprofloxacin 500mg twice daily for 7–14 days OR Azithromycin 500mg once daily for 7 days
Typhoid Fever.explanation: Typhoid is caused by Salmonella Typhi via contaminated food/water. Ciprofloxacin or Azithromycin are first-line antibiotics. Supportive hydration and rest are critical. Drug resistance is rising — culture before treating if possible.
lifestyle: Drink only boiled or purified water
Eat freshly cooked food — avoid raw vegetables and street food
Complete full antibiotic course even if feeling better
Wash hands thoroughly before eating and after toilet
Sponge bath if very high fever (above 104°F)
Recheck stools/blood culture after treatment completion
Typhoid Fever.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency for intestinal perforation signs: sudden severe abdominal pain, rigid abdomen, high fever with confusion or collapse.

Malaria.dosage: 4 tablets at 0, 8, 24, 36, 48, 60 hours (weight-based) — take with fatty food for absorption
Malaria.explanation: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium parasites (falciparum most dangerous) via Anopheles mosquito bite. Artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT) is the gold standard. Severe malaria requires IV Artesunate. Chloroquine for Vivax where sensitive.
lifestyle: Complete the full malaria treatment course without interruption
Drink ORS and fluids generously to prevent dehydration
Use insecticide-treated bed nets every night
Use Permethrin-treated clothing and DEET repellent outdoors
Eliminate stagnant water sources around your home
Monitor blood smear after treatment to confirm clearance
Malaria.when_to_seek_care: EMERGENCY: Severe malaria — cerebral malaria, seizures, altered consciousness, respiratory distress, jaundice, haemoglobinuria (black water fever) — requires IV treatment in ICU.

Pneumonia.duration: 5–7 days (full antibiotic course must be completed)
Pneumonia.explanation: Azithromycin is a broad-spectrum antibiotic effective for community-acquired pneumonia. Complete the full course even if feeling better.
lifestyle: Complete absolute bed rest for 5–7 days
Drink 8–10 glasses of water daily to loosen mucus
Eat high-protein food — eggs, dal, chicken broth
Do NOT stop antibiotics early even if symptoms improve
Practice deep breathing exercises to aid lung recovery
Avoid smoking and second-hand smoke completely
Pneumonia.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency if breathing worsens, lips turn blue, chest pain is severe, or oxygen levels drop below 94%.

Asthma.dosage: 2 puffs every 4–6 hours as needed; Budesonide 200mcg inhaler twice daily for control
Asthma.duration: Ongoing management — consult pulmonologist for long-term plan
Asthma.explanation: Salbutamol (SABA) relieves acute bronchospasm immediately. Budesonide (ICS) reduces ongoing airway inflammation. Avoid known triggers. Never use inhaler without spacer if possible.
lifestyle: Identify and avoid asthma triggers (dust, pollen, cold air, smoke)
Use inhaler with spacer for maximum effectiveness
Keep rescue inhaler available at all times
Monitor peak flow daily if prescribed a peak flow meter
Exercise in warm environments; avoid cold air exercise
Create an Asthma Action Plan with your doctor
Asthma.when_to_seek_care: Call emergency if: severe breathlessness unable to speak in full sentences, blue lips/fingernails, rescue inhaler provides no relief — this is a life-threatening attack.

Anemia.dosage: Ferrous Sulfate 325mg twice daily with vitamin C | Folic Acid 5mg once daily
Anemia.duration: 3–6 months (until iron stores replenish — monitor Hb monthly)
Anemia.explanation: Iron deficiency anemia requires iron supplementation with vitamin C to enhance absorption. Folic acid treats megaloblastic anemia. Dietary iron from red meat, spinach, lentils supports recovery.
lifestyle: Eat iron-rich foods: red meat, spinach, dates, lentils, beans
Take iron tablet with orange juice (vitamin C doubles absorption)
Avoid drinking tea/coffee within 1 hour of iron tablet
Avoid calcium supplements and antacids at the same time as iron
Cook in iron vessels to add dietary iron
Deworm if living in endemic areas (pinworm/hookworm cause iron loss)
Anemia.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if haemoglobin < 7 g/dL, you experience chest pain, fainting spells, or do not respond to oral iron supplementation after 4 weeks.

Hypertension.dosage: 5mg once daily in the morning — dose may be titrated to 10mg
Hypertension.duration: Lifelong management with regular BP monitoring
Hypertension.explanation: Amlodipine reduces peripheral vascular resistance and lowers blood pressure. Losartan/Telmisartan (ARBs) are alternatives. Lifestyle changes are equally critical. Regular BP monitoring is essential.
lifestyle: Reduce salt intake to < 5g/day — avoid pickles, chips, processed food
DASH diet: fruits, vegetables, whole grains, low-fat dairy
Exercise 30 minutes of brisk walking daily
Quit smoking and limit alcohol strictly
Manage stress — meditation, yoga, 7–8 hours sleep
Monitor BP at home twice daily
Hypertension.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency if BP > 180/120 mmHg (hypertensive crisis), severe headache, blurred vision, chest pain, or neurological symptoms develop.

Gastritis.dosage: Omeprazole 20mg once daily before breakfast | Antacid (Gelusil) after meals as needed
Gastritis.duration: 4–8 weeks (H. pylori — triple therapy 14 days if tested positive)
Gastritis.explanation: Gastritis involves inflammation of the stomach lining, often due to H. pylori, NSAIDs, or stress. Omeprazole (PPI) reduces acid production. H. pylori requires triple therapy (PPI + Amoxicillin + Clarithromycin).
lifestyle: Avoid NSAIDs (Ibuprofen, Aspirin) — switch to Paracetamol
Eat small, frequent meals — avoid long gaps
Avoid spicy, oily, acidic food and carbonated drinks
Do not eat within 2 hours of sleeping — elevate head of bed
Quit smoking and alcohol — both worsen gastritis
Reduce stress — it significantly increases acid production
Gastritis.when_to_seek_care: Seek care for black tarry stools, vomiting blood, weight loss, or persistent pain unresponsive to antacids — these suggest bleeding ulcer.

Migraine.dosage: Sumatriptan 50mg at headache onset (max 200mg/day) | Topiramate 25mg twice daily preventive
Migraine.duration: Sumatriptan: as needed (max 2x per week) | Topiramate: 6–12 months preventive
Migraine.explanation: Sumatriptan is a serotonin receptor agonist that aborts migraine by causing vasoconstriction and blocking trigeminal pain. Topiramate prevents chronic migraines. Avoid triptans if cardiac risk. Identify and avoid personal triggers.
lifestyle: Maintain a migraine diary — identify personal triggers (foods, stress, sleep)
Avoid common triggers: red wine, aged cheese, MSG, skipping meals
Maintain regular sleep schedule — same wake time daily
Apply cold compress and rest in dark, quiet room during attack
Practice regular aerobic exercise — reduces migraine frequency by 50%
Manage stress with yoga, meditation, or cognitive behavioural therapy
Migraine.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency for 'thunderclap' worst headache of life (may indicate subarachnoid hemorrhage), headache with fever+stiff neck (meningitis), new neurological deficits, or headache after head injury.

Tension Headache.dosage: Ibuprofen 400mg every 6–8 hours with food | Paracetamol 500mg every 6 hours
Tension Headache.duration: 1–3 days (limit analgesic use to < 15 days/month to avoid medication overuse headache)
Tension Headache.explanation: Tension headaches are caused by muscle contraction in scalp, neck, and shoulders — often triggered by stress, poor posture, or screen time. NSAIDs and Paracetamol provide relief. If > 15 days/month, amitriptyline prophylaxis is indicated.
lifestyle: Take regular breaks from screen work — 20-20-20 rule (every 20 min, look 20ft away for 20 sec)
Improve posture — ergonomic chair and monitor at eye level
Regular neck and shoulder stretching exercises
Apply warm compress to neck and temple area
Practice relaxation techniques — deep breathing, progressive muscle relaxation
Ensure 7–8 hours of regular sleep nightly
Tension Headache.when_to_seek_care: Seek care if headaches occur daily, worsen progressively, change in character, are accompanied by vision changes, or do not respond to standard analgesics.

Cluster Headache.dosage: O2 15 L/min via non-rebreather mask for 15 minutes | Sumatriptan 6mg SC injection (fastest onset)
Cluster Headache.duration: Acute attacks 15–30 min | Verapamil 80mg 3x daily for cluster prevention during active period
Cluster Headache.explanation: Cluster headaches are the most severe primary headache disorder — excruciating periorbital pain with autonomic features (tearing, congestion, ptosis). Oxygen and SC Sumatriptan are most effective acute treatments. Verapamil prevents cluster bouts.
lifestyle: Completely avoid alcohol during a cluster period — it is the most consistent trigger
Maintain strict regular sleep patterns — sleep deprivation triggers attacks
Avoid bright light and strong smells during cluster period
Do not smoke — nicotine is a major cluster trigger
Keep emergency Sumatriptan injection/nasal spray on hand at all times
Inform workplace/family about condition — attacks are incapacitating
Cluster Headache.when_to_seek_care: All cluster headache patients should see a neurologist. Seek emergency if new neurological symptoms accompany headache — secondary causes must be excluded by imaging.

Sinus Headache.dosage: Amoxicillin 500mg 3x daily for 7 days | Xylometazoline 2 drops each nostril twice daily (max 5 days)
Sinus Headache.explanation: Bacterial sinusitis causes facial pain, blocked nose, and purulent nasal discharge. Amoxicillin treats bacterial infection. Nasal decongestants provide symptomatic relief. Saline irrigation helps clear sinuses.
lifestyle: Saline nasal irrigation (Neti pot) twice daily — improves drainage
Steam inhalation for 10 minutes 3x daily with menthol
Elevate head while sleeping to improve sinus drainage
Stay well hydrated to thin mucus
Use cool mist humidifier in bedroom
Identify and treat underlying allergies if recurrent sinusitis
Sinus Headache.when_to_seek_care: Seek care for vision changes, swollen eye/cheek, high fever, severe headache worsening daily — these suggest orbital or intracranial complications.

UTI.dosage: Nitrofurantoin 100mg twice daily for 5 days | Trimethoprim 200mg twice daily for 3 days
UTI.duration: 3–7 days (uncomplicated UTI) | 10–14 days for complicated UTI
UTI.explanation: UTI in women is most commonly caused by E. coli from the perineal region. Nitrofurantoin concentrates in urine and is effective for uncomplicated cystitis. Ciprofloxacin for complicated or recurrent UTI. Culture and sensitivity testing is recommended.
lifestyle: Drink 2–3 litres of water daily to flush bacteria from urinary tract
Urinate immediately after sexual intercourse
Wipe front to back after toilet use (prevents E. coli spread)
Wear cotton underwear and loose-fitting clothes
Avoid bubble baths, feminine hygiene sprays, and irritants
Cranberry juice/supplements may reduce recurrence risk
UTI.when_to_seek_care: Seek care for flank pain (kidney involvement/pyelonephritis), fever > 101°F, shaking chills, nausea/vomiting with UTI — these indicate ascending infection requiring IV antibiotics.

COVID-19.dosage: Paracetamol 500mg every 6 hours | Vitamin C 1000mg daily | Zinc 50mg daily | Pulse oximetry monitoring
COVID-19.explanation: Mild COVID-19 is managed with symptomatic treatment, rest, and isolation. Monitor oxygen saturation (SpO2) twice daily — if < 94% seek emergency. Severe cases require Remdesivir (antiviral) and Dexamethasone (reduces cytokine storm). Vaccination prevents severe disease.
lifestyle: Isolate from household members — wear N95/surgical mask indoors
Monitor SpO2 twice daily with pulse oximeter — alert if < 94%
Prone position (sleep on stomach) if SpO2 borderline — improves oxygenation
Eat nutritious food — high protein, vitamin C rich fruits
Stay well hydrated — 2–3 L fluids per day
Complete 10-day isolation even if symptoms resolve — you remain infectious
COVID-19.when_to_seek_care: EMERGENCY: SpO2 < 93%, severe breathlessness, unable to complete sentences, confusion, chest pain, or lips turning blue — call emergency services immediately.

Hypothyroidism.dosage: Start 25–50mcg once daily on empty stomach (30 min before breakfast) — titrate by TSH levels every 6–8 weeks
Hypothyroidism.duration: Lifelong daily medication with 6-monthly TSH monitoring
Hypothyroidism.explanation: Hypothyroidism (underactive thyroid) requires lifelong levothyroxine replacement. The dose is adjusted based on TSH levels to maintain normal thyroid function. Hashimoto's thyroiditis is the most common cause.
lifestyle: Take levothyroxine FIRST THING in morning — 30 min before any food or drink
Do NOT take with calcium, iron, or antacids — space 4 hours apart
Regular TSH monitoring every 6 months once stable
Eat selenium-rich foods (Brazil nuts, tuna) — supports thyroid
Regular exercise — hypothyroidism causes weight gain and sluggishness
Track symptoms: fatigue, cold intolerance, constipation — report changes
Hypothyroidism.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency for myxedema coma — extreme hypothyroid state with confusion, hypothermia, and loss of consciousness. Seek care for chest pain, heart palpitations during treatment.

Hyperthyroidism.dosage: Methimazole 10–30mg daily (divided doses) | Propranolol 40mg twice daily for symptom control
Hyperthyroidism.duration: 12–18 months antithyroid therapy — with 3-monthly TFT monitoring
Hyperthyroidism.explanation: Hyperthyroidism (overactive thyroid — most commonly Graves disease) is treated with antithyroid drugs (Methimazole/PTU) to block thyroid hormone synthesis. Propranolol controls palpitations and tremors. Radioiodine or surgery for definitive treatment.
lifestyle: Avoid iodine-rich foods (seaweed, iodized salt excess) — worsens hyperthyroidism
Do NOT use stimulants — caffeine, energy drinks exacerbate palpitations
Rest — hyperthyroidism causes catabolism and weight loss
Eat calorie-dense food to compensate for high metabolic rate
Use sunglasses if Graves ophthalmopathy (eye involvement)
Regular TFT (T3/T4/TSH) monitoring every 3 months
Hyperthyroidism.when_to_seek_care: Emergency for thyroid storm — extreme high fever, confusion, rapid heart rate > 140, vomiting — this is life-threatening and requires ICU care.

Skin Allergy.dosage: Cetirizine 10mg once daily at bedtime | Hydrocortisone cream thin layer 2x daily (max 7 days on face)
Skin Allergy.duration: 5–7 days acute | Identify and avoid trigger for long-term management
Skin Allergy.explanation: Skin allergies (urticaria, contact dermatitis, eczema) are triggered by allergens activating mast cells and releasing histamine. Cetirizine blocks histamine. Topical corticosteroids reduce local inflammation. Identifying and avoiding the trigger is key.
lifestyle: Identify and eliminate contact allergen — soaps, nickel, latex, detergents
Use hypoallergenic, fragrance-free skincare products
Moisturize skin 2–3 times daily with fragrance-free emollient
Avoid hot water baths — use lukewarm water only
Wear loose cotton clothing — avoid synthetic fabric contact
Keep a symptom diary to identify foods or substances causing reactions
Skin Allergy.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency for anaphylaxis — swelling of lips/tongue/throat, difficulty breathing, dizziness with rash — administer Epinephrine (EpiPen) and call emergency services immediately.

Gastroenteritis.dosage: ORS 200ml after each loose stool | Zinc 20mg daily for 10–14 days | Ondansetron 4mg 3x daily for vomiting
Gastroenteritis.explanation: Gastroenteritis involves inflammation of stomach and intestines causing vomiting and diarrhea. Rehydration with ORS is the cornerstone of treatment. Zinc reduces severity and duration. Ondansetron controls vomiting. Antibiotics only for bacterial/parasitic causes.
lifestyle: Drink ORS continuously in small sips — don't wait for thirst
BRAT diet initially: Bananas, Rice, Applesauce, Toast — easy on stomach
Introduce yoghourt/probiotics after 24 hours to restore gut flora
Avoid dairy, spicy, fatty food until full recovery
Wash hands thoroughly after every toilet visit
Isolate from family especially infants/elderly — highly contagious
Gastroenteritis.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency for signs of severe dehydration: dry mouth, no urination for 8 hours, sunken eyes, extreme weakness — IV fluids needed. Blood in stools requires immediate evaluation.

Diabetes Type 2.dosage: 500mg twice daily with meals — titrate to 1000mg twice daily over 4 weeks
Diabetes Type 2.duration: Lifelong with HbA1c monitoring every 3 months
Diabetes Type 2.explanation: Type 2 diabetes is the most common form — insulin resistance and progressive beta-cell dysfunction. Metformin reduces hepatic glucose output and improves insulin sensitivity. Lifestyle changes are equally critical. Add SGLT2 inhibitor or GLP-1 agonist if needed.
lifestyle: Reduce refined carbohydrates (white rice, sugar, maida) — switch to whole grains
Exercise 150 minutes per week — walking, cycling, swimming
Monitor blood sugar before and 2 hours after meals
Target HbA1c < 7% — test every 3 months
Lose 5–10% body weight if overweight — dramatically improves glucose control
Annual eye, kidney, and foot examinations — prevent diabetic complications
Diabetes Type 2.when_to_seek_care: Seek emergency for hypoglycemia (sugar < 70 mg/dL) — confusion, shaking, sweating. Also emergency for DKA — vomiting, fruity breath, extreme weakness, Kussmaul respiration.

Diabetes Type 1.dosage: Glargine 10–20 units once at bedtime | Lispro 5–10 units before each meal (dose individualised)
Diabetes Type 1.duration: Lifelong insulin therapy — dose adjusted by blood glucose readings
Diabetes Type 1.explanation: Type 1 diabetes is an autoimmune condition causing absolute insulin deficiency. Insulin is the only treatment. Basal-bolus regimen mimics natural insulin secretion. Continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) improves outcomes. DKA is the life-threatening emergency.
lifestyle: Monitor blood glucose 4–8 times daily (before meals, bedtime, overnight)
Always carry fast-acting glucose (glucose tablets, juice) for hypoglycemia
Rotate injection sites to prevent lipodystrophy
Maintain consistent meal timing and carbohydrate counting
Wear medical alert bracelet/ID indicating Type 1 Diabetes
Join a structured diabetes education program (DSMES)
Diabetes Type 1.when_to_seek_care: Emergency for DKA — fruity breath, extreme thirst, confusion, abdominal pain, vomiting, blood sugar > 250 with ketones in urine. Also emergency for blood sugar < 50 mg/dL with altered consciousness.

Prediabetes.drug: Metformin 500mg (if high risk) + Lifestyle as primary treatment
Prediabetes.dosage: Metformin 500mg once daily (high-risk individuals); Lifestyle alone for most
Prediabetes.duration: Indefinite lifestyle modification — retest HbA1c every 6 months
Prediabetes.explanation: Prediabetes (HbA1c 5.7–6.4% or fasting glucose 100–125 mg/dL) can often be reversed or delayed with intensive lifestyle intervention. Metformin reduces progression by 31% in high-risk individuals. Weight loss of 7% is the primary target.
lifestyle: Lose 7% of body weight — the most effective intervention to prevent diabetes
Exercise 150 minutes per week (brisk walking, cycling)
Replace refined carbs with whole grains, vegetables, legumes
Eliminate sugary beverages — switch to water, green tea
Sleep 7–8 hours — sleep deprivation worsens insulin resistance
Dietary Program: Mediterranean or low-glycaemic index diet
Prediabetes.when_to_seek_care: Follow up with doctor every 6 months to monitor HbA1c. If HbA1c rises above 6.5%, progression to Type 2 diabetes has occurred.

Knee Pain.dosage: Ibuprofen 400mg with food every 8 hours for up to 5 days. Apply Diclofenac gel directly to knee 3 times daily.
Knee Pain.duration: 5-7 days for acute pain. If chronic (>2 weeks), physiotherapy evaluation needed.
Knee Pain.explanation: Knee pain arises from inflammation of the joint, ligament strains, cartilage damage, or overuse. NSAIDs reduce inflammation and relieve pain. Topical Diclofenac provides direct anti-inflammatory action without systemic effects.
lifestyle: RICE: Rest, Ice (20 min every 2h for first 48h), Compression, Elevation
Avoid deep squatting, running, and high-impact activities
Gentle quad strengthening exercises after acute phase
Use a knee support/brace when walking
Lose weight if overweight — every kg reduces 4kg force on knee
Knee Pain.when_to_seek_care: See a doctor if: pain is severe/non-weight-bearing, sudden swelling within 2h of injury, locking/giving way, or no improvement after 7 days of rest.
alternatives: Ibuprofen gel instead of oral if GI-sensitive
Knee Pain.emergency: Sudden severe swelling + fever + hot joint → possible septic arthritis → Emergency

Lower Back Pain.dosage: Ibuprofen 400mg with food every 8 hours for 5 days. Diclofenac gel apply to lower back 3x daily.
Lower Back Pain.duration: Acute: 5-7 days. Chronic (>6 weeks): physiotherapy needed.
Lower Back Pain.explanation: Lower back pain is most commonly caused by muscle spasm, disc bulge, or poor posture. NSAIDs reduce inflammation and speed recovery. Bed rest for >2 days worsens outcomes — staying gently active is better.
lifestyle: Gentle walking every hour
Maintain neutral spine — avoid prolonged sitting
Heat pad after 48h helps muscle spasm
Strengthen core muscles (physiotherapy)
Ergonomic chair and desk setup
Lower Back Pain.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: radiating pain down both legs, bladder/bowel loss, numbness in genital area (cauda equina emergency), or no improvement in 2 weeks.
Lower Back Pain.emergency: Loss of bladder/bowel control + back pain = Cauda Equina Syndrome — EMERGENCY

Shoulder Pain.dosage: 400mg with food every 8 hours for 5-7 days.
Shoulder Pain.duration: Acute pain: 5-7 days. Frozen shoulder resolves in 12-24 months with physiotherapy.
Shoulder Pain.explanation: Shoulder pain is commonly caused by rotator cuff injury, frozen shoulder (adhesive capsulitis), or impingement. Anti-inflammatory treatment and specific physiotherapy exercises are the mainstay of treatment.
lifestyle: Pendulum exercises gently 3x/day
Avoid carrying heavy bags on affected side
Apply ice 20 min after activity in acute phase
Physiotherapy: specific rotator cuff exercises
Do not sleep on affected shoulder
Shoulder Pain.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: cannot lift arm above head, pain after trauma, fever + swollen joint, or no improvement in 3 weeks.

Neck Pain.dosage: 400mg with food every 8 hours for 5 days.
Neck Pain.duration: Acute: 5-7 days. Cervical spondylosis: long-term physiotherapy.
Neck Pain.explanation: Neck pain most commonly results from poor posture (desk work, phone use), muscle spasm, or cervical disc disease. NSAIDs relieve inflammation. Posture correction and neck mobility exercises accelerate recovery.
lifestyle: Computer screen at eye level
Take break every 30 min — chin tucks
Heat pad for muscle spasm
Avoid phone use while lying down
Cervical collar only for short-term acute use
Neck Pain.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: pain spreads into arm with numbness, tingling, weakness; sudden severe neck pain after trauma; fever + stiff neck (possible meningitis — EMERGENCY).

Ankle Sprain.dosage: 400mg with food every 8 hours for 5 days.
Ankle Sprain.duration: Grade 1: 1-2 weeks. Grade 2: 3-6 weeks. Grade 3: 3-6 months (may need surgery).
Ankle Sprain.explanation: Ankle sprains occur when ligaments are overstretched or torn during rolling or twisting. RICE protocol is the first-line treatment. Early controlled movement improves recovery.
lifestyle: RICE immediately: Rest, Ice (20 min every 2h), Compression bandage, Elevate above heart
Crutches if cannot bear weight
Begin gentle range of motion exercises after 48h
Proprioception balance exercises when pain allows
Avoid returning to sport too early
Ankle Sprain.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: cannot bear any weight, visible deformity, severe swelling, or no improvement in 5 days — X-ray to exclude fracture.

Muscle Strain.dosage: 400mg with food every 8 hours for 5 days.
Muscle Strain.duration: Grade 1: 1-2 weeks. Grade 2: 4-6 weeks. Complete tear: 3-6 months.
Muscle Strain.explanation: Muscle strains happen when muscle fibres are overstretched or torn. RICE protocol and NSAIDs are the first-line treatment. Gentle stretching after 48h helps recovery.
lifestyle: RICE for first 48-72 hours
Gentle stretching from day 3 (pain-free range)
Return to activity gradually
Warm up properly before exercise in future
Hydration and magnesium for cramp prevention
Muscle Strain.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: sudden severe pain + pop sound + visible deformity (possible complete tear) or no improvement in 2 weeks.

Sciatica.dosage: 400mg with food every 8 hours for 5-7 days.
Sciatica.duration: Acute episode: 4-6 weeks. Chronic: 3-6 months physiotherapy.
Sciatica.explanation: Sciatica is caused by compression of the sciatic nerve, usually from a herniated disc in the lower back. Pain radiates from lower back through the buttock and down the leg. NSAIDs reduce nerve inflammation.
lifestyle: Walking and gentle movement — avoid bed rest
McKenzie exercises for disc herniation
Avoid sitting for prolonged periods
Ice or heat to lower back alternately
Sleep on side with pillow between knees
Sciatica.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: both legs affected, bladder/bowel incontinence, progressive leg weakness, or no improvement in 4 weeks. These may indicate cauda equina — EMERGENCY.
Sciatica.emergency: Bilateral leg weakness + bladder/bowel loss = Cauda Equina — Go to ER NOW

Osteoarthritis.dosage: 500-1000mg every 6 hours, maximum 4g/day. Topical Diclofenac gel as alternative.
Osteoarthritis.explanation: Osteoarthritis is the most common form of arthritis, caused by cartilage breakdown in joints (especially knees, hips, hands). It is a chronic condition managed with pain relief, exercise, weight loss, and eventually joint replacement.
lifestyle: Low-impact exercise: swimming, cycling, walking
Lose weight (each kg lost = 4kg less knee force)
Physiotherapy for strengthening muscles around joint
Heat for chronic pain, ice for flare-ups
Walking aids (stick/walker) if needed
Osteoarthritis.when_to_seek_care: Refer rheumatology or orthopaedics if: severe joint deformity, loss of function affecting daily life, or considering joint replacement.

Carpal Tunnel Syndrome.dosage: 400mg with food every 8 hours for 7 days (short-term flare).
Carpal Tunnel Syndrome.duration: Wrist splint: 6-12 weeks. Surgery if conservative fails.
Carpal Tunnel Syndrome.explanation: Carpal Tunnel Syndrome is caused by compression of the median nerve at the wrist, causing numbness and tingling in the thumb, index, and middle fingers. Common in office workers and pregnant women.
lifestyle: Wear wrist splint at night (maintains neutral wrist position)
Avoid repetitive wrist flexion
Take regular breaks from typing/computer
Ergonomic keyboard and mouse position
Vitamin B6 supplement (discuss with doctor)
Carpal Tunnel Syndrome.when_to_seek_care: Refer to specialist if: muscle wasting at thumb base, constant numbness, or symptoms not controlled after 6 weeks of splinting — consider steroid injection or surgery.

Tennis Elbow.dosage: Diclofenac gel apply to elbow 3-4x daily. Ibuprofen 400mg every 8h with food for 5-7 days if needed.
Tennis Elbow.duration: 6 weeks to 2 years. Most resolve with physiotherapy.
Tennis Elbow.explanation: Tennis elbow (lateral epicondylitis) is caused by overuse of forearm muscles, causing pain at the outer elbow. Common in people who use a computer mouse, rackets, or perform repetitive manual work.
lifestyle: Rest from aggravating activity
Ice the elbow for 20 min 3x daily
Eccentric wrist extension exercises (physiotherapy)
Use tennis elbow clasp/strap when active
Ergonomic mouse and keyboard setup
Tennis Elbow.when_to_seek_care: Refer physiotherapy. See doctor if no improvement after 6 weeks — consider steroid injection or shock wave therapy.

Plantar Fasciitis.dosage: 400mg with food every 8 hours for 7 days.
Plantar Fasciitis.duration: 6-12 months with proper stretching and footwear.
Plantar Fasciitis.explanation: Plantar fasciitis is inflammation of the thick band of tissue that runs under the foot, causing heel pain — worst in the morning when taking first steps.
lifestyle: Calf and plantar fascia stretch before getting out of bed
Wear supportive footwear — avoid flat shoes, bare feet on hard floors
Ice the heel 20 min after activity
Orthotic arch support insoles
Lose weight if overweight
Plantar Fasciitis.when_to_seek_care: See podiatrist or orthopaedics if no improvement after 6 months — consider steroid injection or shock wave therapy.

Conjunctivitis.dosage: 1 drop in affected eye every 2 hours while awake for 2 days, then every 4 hours for 5 days.
Conjunctivitis.explanation: Conjunctivitis (red eye) is inflammation of the conjunctiva, most commonly caused by bacterial infection (producing discharge), viral infection, or allergy. Bacterial type responds to antibiotic drops.
lifestyle: Wash hands frequently — highly contagious
Do not share towels or pillowcases
Remove contact lenses until resolved
Cool compress for comfort
Avoid rubbing eyes
Conjunctivitis.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: vision loss, severe eye pain, cloudy cornea, no improvement after 5 days of drops, or newborn with eye discharge (within first month — urgent).

Ear Infection.dosage: Adult: 500mg every 8 hours for 5 days. Child: 40mg/kg/day in 3 divided doses for 5 days.
Ear Infection.explanation: Ear infections (otitis media) are most common in children, often following an upper respiratory infection. Bacteria (Streptococcus, Haemophilus) invade the middle ear, causing ear pain, fever, and hearing loss.
lifestyle: Pain relief: Paracetamol every 6h (age appropriate)
Warm pad over ear for comfort
Keep ear dry — no swimming
Exclusive breastfeeding reduces ear infection risk in infants
Complete the antibiotic course even if better
Ear Infection.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: discharge from ear, fever persisting beyond 48 hours on antibiotics, child appears very unwell, or repeated ear infections (ENT referral).

Sinusitis.drug: Nasal saline rinse + Cetirizine 10mg (allergic) or Amoxicillin 500mg (bacterial)
Sinusitis.dosage: Amoxicillin 500mg every 8h for 7 days if bacterial. Cetirizine 10mg once daily for allergic. Nasal saline rinse 2-3x daily.
Sinusitis.duration: Acute: 7-10 days. Chronic (>12 weeks): ENT referral.
Sinusitis.explanation: Sinusitis is inflammation of the paranasal sinuses, usually following a viral cold (viral sinusitis needs no antibiotics). Bacterial sinusitis causes facial pain, thick yellow/green discharge, and fever. Nasal saline rinses are highly effective.
lifestyle: Steam inhalation 10 min twice daily
Saline nasal rinse (Neti pot or Nasobal)
Stay hydrated
Avoid cigarette smoke and allergens
Elevate head of bed
Sinusitis.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: fever > 39°C, severe facial pain, swelling around eye (possible orbital cellulitis — URGENT), or no improvement after 10 days.

Acid Reflux.dosage: 40mg once daily, 30 minutes before breakfast for 4 weeks.
Acid Reflux.explanation: Acid reflux (GERD) occurs when stomach acid flows back into the oesophagus, causing heartburn and regurgitation. Proton pump inhibitors (PPIs) reduce acid production and heal the oesophageal lining.
lifestyle: Avoid trigger foods: caffeine, alcohol, spicy/fatty food, chocolate, mint
Eat smaller meals — avoid large meals before bed
Raise head of bed by 20-30cm
Lose weight if overweight
Do not lie down within 3h of eating
Acid Reflux.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: difficulty swallowing, weight loss, vomiting blood, chest pain (rule out cardiac), or symptoms persist despite 8 weeks of PPI therapy.

Irritable Bowel Syndrome.duration: 4-8 weeks (reassess). IBS is a long-term condition needing lifestyle modification.
Irritable Bowel Syndrome.explanation: IBS is a functional disorder of the gut characterised by abdominal pain, bloating, and alternating diarrhoea/constipation. Mebeverine relaxes intestinal smooth muscle to reduce spasms.
lifestyle: Low-FODMAP diet under dietitian guidance
Eat regular meals — do not skip
Reduce caffeine and alcohol
Exercise regularly to regulate bowel
Stress management — mindfulness, CBT
Irritable Bowel Syndrome.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: blood in stool, unintentional weight loss, family history of bowel cancer, or symptoms starting after age 45 — colonoscopy to exclude organic disease.

Constipation.dosage: 15ml twice daily initially, adjust to 1-2 soft stools/day.
Constipation.duration: Short-term until bowel habit normalised. Address root cause.
Constipation.explanation: Constipation occurs when bowel movements are infrequent or difficult. It is commonly caused by low fibre diet, dehydration, inactivity, and medications (iron, opioids). Lactulose is a safe osmotic laxative.
lifestyle: Increase dietary fibre (30g/day): fruit, veg, wholegrains
Drink 2L water/day
Exercise daily — even walking stimulates bowel
Respond to urge to defecate immediately
Correct position on toilet (squatting angle)
Constipation.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: blood in stool, unexplained weight loss, constipation alternating with diarrhoea (possible bowel cancer), or no improvement with laxatives after 2 weeks.

Tonsillitis.dosage: 500mg every 8 hours for 10 days (bacterial strep). Child: 40mg/kg/day in 3 doses.
Tonsillitis.duration: 10 days full course to prevent rheumatic fever.
Tonsillitis.explanation: Tonsillitis is inflammation of the tonsils caused by bacterial (Group A Streptococcus) or viral infection. Bacterial tonsillitis requires antibiotics to prevent complications including rheumatic fever.
lifestyle: Adequate rest and hydration
Paracetamol or Ibuprofen for pain and fever
Gargle warm salt water
Soft diet — ice cream soothing
Complete antibiotic course even if better
Tonsillitis.when_to_seek_care: See doctor/ENT if: recurring tonsillitis (5+ times/year), peritonsillar abscess (quinsy — asymmetric swelling pushing uvula to one side), or difficulty breathing/swallowing — URGENT.

Insomnia.dosage: 2mg 30-60 minutes before bed. Short-term use (max 4 weeks).
Insomnia.duration: Address root cause. Sleep hygiene should be primary intervention.
Insomnia.explanation: Insomnia is difficulty falling or staying asleep. It is most commonly caused by stress, anxiety, poor sleep hygiene, or irregular circadian rhythm. Melatonin helps synchronise the sleep-wake cycle for sleep-onset insomnia.
lifestyle: Fixed sleep and wake time every day
No screens 1h before bed (blue light reduces melatonin)
Dark, cool, quiet bedroom
No caffeine after 2pm
Wind-down routine: reading, bath, mindfulness
Insomnia.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: insomnia lasting >3 months, daytime impairment, snoring + apnoeas (possible sleep apnoea — sleep study), or underlying anxiety/depression needing treatment.

Vertigo.dosage: 25mg 3 times daily. For BPPV — Epley manoeuvre is curative and preferred over long-term medication.
Vertigo.duration: Until symptoms resolve. BPPV usually resolves with Epley manoeuvre in 1-3 sessions.
Vertigo.explanation: Vertigo is the sensation that the room is spinning, caused by inner ear issues (BPPV, labyrinthitis, vestibular neuritis) or rarely central causes. BPPV (commonest) responds dramatically to the Epley manoeuvre.
lifestyle: Epley manoeuvre (BPPV) — perform with guidance
Move slowly when changing position
Avoid sudden head movements
Stay hydrated and avoid salt if Meniere's
Drive only when fully symptom-free
Vertigo.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: sudden onset with headache + new neurological symptoms (possible stroke — FAST protocol). Vertigo + facial drooping/arm weakness = EMERGENCY.

Haemorrhoids.dosage: Apply to affected area 3 times daily and after each bowel movement.
Haemorrhoids.duration: 4-6 weeks for mild-moderate. Persistent cases need surgical evaluation.
Haemorrhoids.explanation: Haemorrhoids (piles) are swollen blood vessels in the rectum/anus causing pain, itching, and rectal bleeding. Most are internal (higher up) or external (at anal opening). Conservative treatment with topical agents and stool softeners is first-line.
lifestyle: High-fibre diet and 2L water/day to prevent straining
Lactulose if constipated
Warm sitz baths 15 min after bowel movement
Do not strain at toilet — respond to urge
Avoid prolonged sitting on toilet
Haemorrhoids.when_to_seek_care: See doctor if: significant rectal bleeding (to rule out bowel cancer especially age>45), persistent severe pain, or prolapsed haemorrhoids — may need banding or surgery.

Anxiety.dosage: 10-40mg as a single dose 30 minutes before anxiety-provoking situation (e.g. presentation).
Anxiety.duration: Short-term situational use only. CBT recommended for generalised anxiety.
Anxiety.explanation: Anxiety is characterised by excessive worry, palpitations, trembling, and fear. Propranolol blocks the physical symptoms (racing heart, trembling). CBT is the evidence-based treatment for generalised anxiety disorder.
lifestyle: Daily exercise: 30 min aerobic
Meditation or mindfulness 10-20 min morning
Limit caffeine and alcohol
Sleep 7-8 hours
Journaling worry thoughts and challenging them
Anxiety.when_to_seek_care: See GP/psychiatrist if: daily anxiety affecting function, panic attacks, depression co-existing, or needing long-term medication — do not self-prescribe anxiolytics.
